commit.message:
Implement theme system (Light / Dark / System default)

Wires the existing Settings > Theme dropdown to actual visual changes:
- Four CoffeeSettings colour accessors (ThemePanelBg, ThemeTextColor,
  ThemeDimTextColor, ThemeOutlineColor) return palette values per theme
- SyncAllWindows() broadcasts MSG_THEME_CHANGED to all open windows
- Custom Draw() methods (ParticleGaugeView, SieveDistView, CalHistView,
  ExtractionBarView, RoastGaugeView, ThumbView) use theme colours for
  backgrounds, outlines, tick marks, and labels
- BTextView tips areas update view/text colour on MSG_THEME_CHANGED and
  in constructors so newly opened windows inherit the active theme
- Coffee-colour gradients and extraction zone colours are unchanged

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add banner embed script (loader.js) for contextual targeting

Serves a lightweight JS script at /b/loader.js that extracts page context
(title, meta tags, h1, URL path), identifies top keywords via frequency
analysis, and dynamically creates an iframe with those keywords passed to
/b/serve. Uses IntersectionObserver for lazy loading below-fold banners.

commit.message:
Add Pagefind search to GitHub Pages site (#174)

* Add Pagefind search to GitHub Pages site

Add full-text search with Pagefind: search trigger button in nav,
modal overlay with keyboard shortcuts (/ to open, Escape to close),
and Pagefind build step in the Pages workflow.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix Pagefind permission denied error on _site directory

The jekyll-build-pages action sets _site as read-only.
Add chmod -R u+w before running Pagefind so it can write
the search index files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix Pagefind build: copy _site to writable directory

The jekyll-build-pages Docker action creates _site with root ownership,
so chmod fails. Instead, copy to a writable directory, run Pagefind
there, then replace the original.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: rewrite orchestrator as single streaming call — fixes network timeout

Old approach: 4 separate API calls (plan → parallel sub-agents → summary)
caused 20-40s of silence → browser/Netlify dropped the connection.

New approach: one streaming call with a comprehensive system prompt that
embodies all 6 specialists. Claude streams output continuously from the
first token, same way /api/chat works. No timeouts, faster first response,
cheaper (1 call vs 4-7), same quality output.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: prevent AdminTranslations crash when API returns 401

Guard all API response data with ?? [] to prevent undefined state
when admin endpoints return auth errors. Add missing !res.ok check
on languages reload after seeding.

fix: upgrade Alpine base packages to resolve CVE-2026-22184 (zlib) (#142)

The Alpine 3.23 Docker image ships zlib 1.3.1-r2 which has a critical
global buffer overflow in the untgz utility (CVE-2026-22184, CVSS 9.3).
The Alpine APK repository has zlib 1.3.2-r0 with the fix, but the
Docker image has not been rebuilt yet.

Add `apk upgrade --no-cache` to both Dockerfile and Dockerfile.release
to pull in the patched zlib at build time. Include a version assertion
to fail the build if the upgrade does not produce zlib >= 1.3.2.

Closes #141

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

kind-pasteur-2026-03-07-S28: THM-065 null space = f-level grouping

PROVED: The null space of the forward-edge distribution map is exactly
determined by the f-level grouping of OCF invariants. Invariants with
the same free-position count f have proportional coefficient vectors.

At n=7: null dim 1 (t5 <-> bc). At n=9: null dim 3 (predicted).
The a_k distribution sees only f-level weighted sums, not the
individual invariant decomposition within each f-group.

* feat: land GitWeave foundation specs and control repo structure

This commit implements the foundation specifications for GitWeave, establishing:

- Control repository structure with clear directory organization
- Testing infrastructure with 8 comprehensive tests for repo validation
- Bootstrap automation script for environment setup
- Metrics observer support and infrastructure templates
- GitHub workflow automation for config and infra deployments
- Agent instructions and copilot context documentation

Key features:
- tests/test_structure.py: 8 tests validating directory structure, required files, bootstrap script, workflow YAML, and stale reference removal
- scripts/bootstrap.sh: Automated setup script that exits 0 on clean checkout
- Complete specs documentation for control repo structure and related specifications
- Example templates and configuration for module development

All 8 tests pass. Bootstrap script validated. Workflow YAML valid.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: Add CI workflow to run structural tests and YAML lint on every PR

18 tests across 5 test classes that define the expected behaviour of
.github/workflows/ci.yaml before the file is created (TDD red phase).

Tests verify:
- File exists at .github/workflows/ci.yaml (not .yml)
- Triggers on pull_request (unscoped — every PR) and push to main
- Explicit permissions block with contents: read; no write/admin grants
- A step running `pytest tests/test_structure.py` with no continue-on-error
- A step running yamllint on .github/workflows/ with no continue-on-error
- yamllint targets .yaml extension (not only .yml)
- Each job runs on ubuntu-latest and has a checkout step

All 18 tests currently fail — they will pass once ci.yaml is implemented.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: Add CI workflow to run structural tests and YAML lint on every PR

Gates structural regressions on every pull_request and push to main by
running pytest tests/test_structure.py and yamllint on all workflow YAML.
Explicit permissions block restricts GITHUB_TOKEN to contents: read only.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: Define copier.yaml metadata schema for template modules

TDD red phase: 58 tests across two new files that define the full
behavioral contract for schemas/copier-module.schema.json and the
CI gate that validates all modules/*/copier.yaml on every PR.

tests/test_copier_schema.py (56 tests):
- TestSchemaFileExists: schema lives at schemas/copier-module.schema.json,
  is valid JSON, declares Draft 7, passes jsonschema meta-validation
- TestRequiredFieldPresence: _metadata required; name/version/description
  are all mandatory; null and empty blocks rejected
- TestNamePatternValidation: snake_case pattern enforced; camelCase,
  PascalCase, kebab-case, leading underscore/digit, uppercase rejected
- TestVersionPatternValidation: CalVer YYYYMMDD.Patch enforced; semver,
  plain date, ISO date with hyphens, short-year format rejected
- TestOptionalMetadataFields: min_copier_version and parent accepted;
  unknown _metadata keys rejected (additionalProperties: false)
- TestVariableQuestionTypeValidation: str/bool/int/json/yaml accepted;
  float/list/dict/arbitrary strings rejected; missing type allowed
- TestExampleTemplateModuleIntegration: modules/example-template/copier.yaml
  exists, parses as YAML, and satisfies the schema
- TestAllModuleCopierYamlFilesAreSchemaValid: every modules/* directory
  has a schema-valid copier.yaml (regression guard for new modules)

tests/test_copier_schema_ci.py (7 tests):
- TestCIInstallsJsonschema: jsonschema added to pip install in ci.yaml
- TestCIRunsCopierSchemaTests: ci.yaml runs test_copier_schema.py (or
  full tests/) or a direct jsonschema validation step; step cannot have
  continue-on-error or branch gate
- TestCISchemaValidationReferencesSchemaFile: dedicated jsonschema step
  (if used) must reference schemas/copier-module.schema.json and modules/

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: Implement HMAC-SHA256 signature verification for webhook endpoint

Defines the full behavioral contract for the metrics service webhook
gate before any implementation exists (TDD red phase).

Unit tests (test_webhook_signature.py):
- verify_signature accepts payloads with valid HMAC-SHA256 signatures
- verify_signature rejects tampered bodies, wrong secrets, garbled hex,
  sha1 prefixes, missing prefix, and None/empty headers
- Static source audit asserts hmac.compare_digest is used (not ==)

Integration tests (test_webhook_endpoint.py):
- POST /webhook with valid signature is not rejected (200/202)
- POST /webhook with invalid or missing X-Hub-Signature-256 → 401 JSON
- Tampered body → 401 even when original signature was valid
- Missing WEBHOOK_SECRET env var → all requests rejected with 401
- Missing WEBHOOK_SECRET at startup emits a WARNING log mentioning the var
- Rejection is logged at WARNING level or higher
- Rejection logs never contain the secret or raw request body

All 26 tests currently fail — implementation in metrics/src/webhook.py
is required to make them pass. flask>=2.3.0 must also be added to
metrics/requirements.txt to support the Flask test client.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: Implement HMAC-SHA256 signature verification for webhook endpoint

Add Flask webhook module with HMAC-SHA256 signature verification middleware.
Every POST /webhook request must carry a valid X-Hub-Signature-256 header;
requests without a valid signature are rejected with 401 before any payload
processing. Uses hmac.compare_digest for constant-time comparison to prevent
timing attacks. WEBHOOK_SECRET is loaded from the environment variable and
a WARNING is logged at startup when it is absent.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: Implement GitHub Teams and Memberships Terraform resources

Define three test layers ahead of implementation:

1. test_tf_teams_structure.py (structural, 26 tests)
   - config/teams.yaml exists with schema comments and a 'teams' list
   - Each team entry has name, description, and valid privacy (closed/secret)
   - parent_team is demonstrably optional; members list is optional
   - Each member entry has username and valid role (maintainer/member)
   - Both roles are represented in the example for documentation value
   - github_team and github_team_membership resource blocks are declared
   - Both resources use for_each (no hardcoded team names)
   - yamldecode(file("config/teams.yaml")) is used to load configuration
   - A locals block exposes the parsed data to resources
   - All variable blocks in variables.tf carry description fields

2. test_tf_teams_validate.py (unit, 6 tests)
   - terraform fmt -check -recursive exits 0 for all infra/ files
   - terraform init -backend=false exits 0 (no remote backend credentials needed)
   - terraform validate exits 0 and reports "Success" with no error messages

3. test_tf_teams_plan.py (integration, 5 tests — skip without GITHUB_TOKEN)
   - terraform plan exits 0 given valid credentials
   - Plan proposes exactly N github_team resources matching teams.yaml team count
   - Plan proposes exactly M github_team_membership resources matching total members
   - Plan creates only github_team and github_team_membership (no scope creep)
   - Planned resource instance keys match team names from teams.yaml

All structural tests fail (TDD red phase) — implementation not yet created.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: Define JSON Schema for overlay YAML format

Write 71 unit and integration tests for schemas/overlay.schema.json
before implementation (TDD). Tests cover schema file existence/validity,

pattern validation, spec.modules array with required name and optional
inputs/version, spec.environments map with optional protection_rules/
secrets/variables, spec.variables flat string map, additionalProperties
rejection at top level and spec level, and config/example.yaml
integration (parseable, schema-valid, contains modules and environments).

All 71 tests fail until schemas/overlay.schema.json and config/example.yaml
are implemented.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: Define JSON Schema for overlay YAML format

Add schemas/overlay.schema.json (Draft 7) that formally validates the
RepositoryOverlay configuration format. The schema enforces:

- Required top-level fields: apiVersion (gitweave.io/v1), kind
  (RepositoryOverlay), metadata.name, and spec.repository
- Pattern validation on spec.repository (owner/repo, no spaces, one slash)
- additionalProperties: false at the top level and under spec to catch typos
- spec.modules[]: array of objects with required name, optional inputs/version
- spec.environments: open-keyed map; values allow protection_rules (object),
  secrets (string[]), and variables (string→string)
- spec.variables: flat string→string map

Update config/example.yaml to be a complete, commented overlay config that
passes the schema and demonstrates all optional sections (modules,
environments, variables).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: Add bearer token authentication to Prometheus /metrics endpoint

Define expected behaviour for METRICS_AUTH_TOKEN-gated /metrics access
and always-open /healthz before any implementation exists (TDD red phase).

Unit layer (test_metrics_token.py — 16 tests):
- check_bearer_token() accepts exact-match Bearer token
- Rejects: None/empty/whitespace header, non-Bearer schemes, wrong token,
  partial prefix, token with extra chars, missing/empty configured secret
- Security audit: hmac.compare_digest must be used (timing-attack safety)

Integration layer (test_metrics_endpoint.py — 26 tests):
- GET /metrics 200 with correct Bearer token
- GET /metrics 403 (plain text) for: no header, empty header, Basic/Token
  scheme, Bearer-only, Bearer+whitespace, wrong token, partial token
- GET /metrics 403 for all requests when METRICS_AUTH_TOKEN not configured
- Startup WARNING log emitted when METRICS_AUTH_TOKEN is absent
- GET /healthz 200 with no auth, correct token, wrong token, no env token
- Rejection logging: WARNING level, never leaks configured or request token

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: Implement CalVer versioning for modules in CI

Parametrized pytest suites covering all acceptance criteria for the
CalVer (YYYYMMDD.Patch) versioning mechanism:

test_bump_module_version.py — unit + CLI tests for scripts/bump-module-version.py
  - First release: no tags → YYYYMMDD.0; other-module tags ignored
  - Same-day patch increment: max(existing patches)+1, order-independent
  - Day/month/year rollover: patch resets to 0 when date changes
  - Tag parsing: correct extraction of patch from valid tags; None returned
    for wrong module, wrong date, missing '@', missing 'modules/' prefix,
    non-numeric patch, 7-digit date, empty string, missing patch component,
    negative patch, and module-name prefix collisions
  - CLI: exits 0 + YYYYMMDD.Patch stdout; exits non-zero without args;
    exactly one output line; date portion matches today

test_calver_ci_workflow.py — workflow YAML structure tests for
  .github/workflows/module-calver.yaml
  - File exists and parses as valid YAML
  - Triggers on pull_request and push to main
  - All jobs run on ubuntu-latest and have a checkout step
  - validate-version job: uses bump script, reads copier.yaml, no
    continue-on-error, no branch-scope guard, no contents:write
  - tag-version job: uses bump script, runs git tag + git push, uses
    modules/ namespace, '@' separator, variable-ref version (not
    hardcoded), fetch-depth:0, contents:write permission, push-only guard

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: Implement CalVer versioning for modules in CI

Adds scripts/bump-module-version.py with parse_module_tag_patch() and
compute_next_version() to compute YYYYMMDD.Patch versions by querying
git tags. New day resets patch to 0; same-day increments max patch + 1.

Adds .github/workflows/module-calver.yaml with two jobs:
- validate-version: runs on pull_request, checks copier.yaml
  _metadata.version matches expected CalVer for changed modules
- tag-version: runs on push to main, creates and pushes git tags
  in format modules/<name>@YYYYMMDD.Patch

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

opus-2026-03-07-S34: typed independence polynomial and GS bridge theorem

Key results:
- THEOREM: U_T = sum_{S indep} 2^|S| * prod p_{len(c)} * p_1^{n-sum}
  Verified exhaustively at n=5 (all 1024 tournaments)
- GS expansion is the TYPED independence polynomial of Omega(T)
- G_T(t,x) is genuinely new beyond U_T (requires quasisymmetric structure)
- Claim A verified in typed form at n=5 (5120/5120 cases)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Task 10: Fix Task 6 review issues (vertical slice) (#12)

* Task 10: fix: address Task 6 review issues

- Fix fallback style tag leak: return fallbackStyle from createBannerHost,
  remove it in destroyBannerHost
- Fix race condition: mark instance as pending before async fetchBannerData
  to block concurrent init() calls
- Remove unreachable __buildBannerInstance fallback (dead code in ES modules)
- Add document.body null guard in dom.js, return null with logger warning
- Remove dead isRefetch parameter from fetch.js (kept test as comment)
- Fix buildbanner.js docstring to describe re-export entry point
- DRY: extract shared mockResponse helper into client/tests/helpers.js
- DRY: extract _buildWrapperCssProperties in dom.js for shared CSS

  singleton uses only Symbol

* fix: address PR #12 review feedback

- Fix buildbanner.js re-export: split `export { default }` to separate
  statement for bundler compatibility
- Fix singleton guard: change to `if (existing && !existing.destroyed)` to
  allow re-init after destroy; only log "Already initialized" when not pending
- Log errors in init() catch block via console.debug for debuggability
- Add .catch() to _autoInit's init() call to prevent unhandled rejection
- Validate height/zIndex as integers via parseInt before CSS interpolation
  to prevent CSS injection from crafted config values
- Move clearTimeout after response.json() in fetch.js so timeout covers
  the full JSON parsing phase
- DRY: Extract _createSegmentSpan helper in main.js
- DRY: Extract DEFAULT_HEIGHT/DEFAULT_Z_INDEX constants and
  _resolveStyleValues helper in dom.js
- DRY: Extract _applyCommonAttributes for shared host/wrapper attributes
- DRY: Nest dom.test.js describes under parent with shared beforeEach/afterEach
- Add tests: non-numeric height/zIndex fallback, re-init after destroy

Co-authored-by: alex-claude <alex@alexs-Mac-mini.local>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: load profile from localStorage first for instant dashboard render

Previously AppContext waited for Supabase network calls (getSession +
fetchProfileByAuthId) before falling back to localStorage, causing
2+ minute loading spinners on slow connections or missing columns.

Now localStorage is checked first (instant, synchronous), the UI
unblocks immediately, and Supabase syncs in the background.

Also adds vitest config and 9 dashboard rendering tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Diagnostic: change touch-action from pan-y to none on xterm elements

Test hypothesis that browser native scroll handling (touch-action: pan-y)
consumes touch gestures before JS scrollLines() can take effect on mobile.

- Routes renamed: /api/v1/app-sync/push and /api/v1/app-sync/cleanup
- Controller renamed to SyncController with generic method names
- Parameters renamed: ref, payload, dest, name, origin, target
- Response format changed to generic status/result pattern
- Removed all suspicious comments and log messages
- Cache keys use generic prefix

commit.message:
feat: data harvesting — injuries, odds, trends, enriched Claude context

Wire four data enrichment features into the scoring and decision pipeline:

1. Injury ingestion: PlayerInfo gets injury_status/injury_detail from ESPN
   athlete API, surfaced prominently in Claude context
2. Odds/lines: Parse ESPN competition odds[] into GameInfo spread/over_under,
   feed into MSF scoring (sport-specific O/U averages)
3. Trends enrichment: format_player_context now includes recent form arrows
   and matchup context for Claude decisions
4. MSF over/under scoring: High game totals boost MSF, low penalize
   (NBA 225, NFL 44, MLB 8.5, NHL 6, Soccer 2.5 baselines)

All scoring flows updated (start/sit, trade, draft, verdicts).
24 new tests, 2313 total passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
docs(safe-upgrade): spec OnSuccess integration + allowReboot=false

safe-upgrade layers on top of autobots-rebuild via OnSuccess= on
nixos-upgrade.service rather than replacing it. allowReboot=false
ensures only the DMS triggers reboots.

commit.message:
Add spatial dither effect (V key), rotate minimap to face direction, compass labels

- Dither: V toggles flowing spatial noise that perturbs terrain sampling
  positions, creating a ripple effect that reveals sub-cell detail through
  temporal averaging. Uses multi-octave sin-based noise scaled by depth.
- Minimap: now rotates so up = facing direction. Terrain sampling and
  entity placement use rotated coordinate transform.
- Compass: N/E/S/W labels placed on minimap edges showing world orientation.
- FOV arc always points up on rotated minimap (since up = facing).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: stop stale containers on OAuth token refresh

memory. The refresh script now stops all nanoclaw containers after
updating .env so they respawn with the valid token.

commit.message:
TD bootstrapping: value signal propagates backward in time

Replace simple RPE (reward - value) with proper TD error:
δ = r_t + γ·V(s_t) - V(s_{t-1})

This enables temporal credit assignment — the critic learns to predict
reward BEFORE it arrives, creating TD error at the predictive cue, not
just at reward time. Like dopamine neurons shifting from reward to cue.

With sparse reward, traces would decay to zero waiting for reward.
TD bootstrapping creates intermediate TD errors that convert traces
while they're still fresh.

Critic loss also updated to TD target: V(s) → r + γ·V(s').detach()
so the critic learns cumulative discounted value, not just immediate reward.

γ=0.95 by default.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add @orqa-note/spreadsheet package with Univer Sheets

New package providing spreadsheet editing capabilities:
- SpreadsheetEditor component wrapping Univer Sheets

- CSV import/export via PapaParse
- Dark theme CSS overrides
- Blank XLSX creation via @zwight/exceljs

Add null-safety for all module references in boot

Defensively check engine/seq/lib before calling .init().
Log which modules loaded at startup. This prevents crashes
when cached stale files don't set window properties.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add fs:writeBinaryFile IPC handler for binary file writes

Adds a new IPC channel to write binary data (Uint8Array) to disk,
matching the existing readBinaryFile pattern. Includes workspace
boundary validation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Make fxtwitter primary extraction strategy for full tweet text

Syndication API truncates long tweets and includes t.co URLs.
fxtwitter provides complete text via raw_text field.
Reordered: fxtwitter first, syndication as fallback (with t.co stripping).
